title: Deliciously Snappy Malaysian Duck Legs Satay
description: A jammy and snappy Malaysian dish featuring duck legs, perfect for an energizing meal, made with whole30-friendly ingredients. This recipe is a twist on traditional satay, using duck legs for a unique flavor and texture.
instructions: Preheat the grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender, combine coconut milk, lime juice, olive oil, ginger paste, garlic paste, salt, and black pepper, and blend until smooth.
Place the duck legs in a large bowl and pour the marinade over them, making sure they are fully coated.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Remove the duck legs from the marinade and grill for 20-25 minutes, or until they reach an internal temperature of 165 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the duck is cooking, prepare the pickle slices by slicing them into thin strips.
Once the duck is cooked, let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing it into thin strips.
Serve the duck with the pickle slices and garnish with chopped fresh cilantro.
